danger_signals.visual_form: Fire, water, earth, and air-themed obstacle visuals corresponding to plane environment
danger_signals.meaning: Immediate environmental threat; platforming hazard that damages or kills player

danger_signals.visual_form: Distinctive enemy sprites at end of each plane level, visually distinct from common enemies
danger_signals.meaning: Major threat encounter; significant danger spike requiring skill

danger_signals.visual_form: H.R. Giger-influenced art direction with dark atmosphere, grotesque character design
danger_signals.meaning: Supernatural evil presence; oppressive, hostile world state

danger_signals.visual_form: Hourglass UI element at bottom of screen draining over time
danger_signals.meaning: Time pressure; failure state if depleted; reinforces futility and circular curse

safety_signals.visual_form: Central platforming area where player returns after level completion or death
safety_signals.meaning: Safe respawn point; allows level selection and recovery between challenges

safety_signals.visual_form: New weapons appear in first stage of each elemental plane (Grappling Hook, Hammer, etc.)
safety_signals.meaning: Progression and player empowerment; toolkit expansion grants access to new areas

safety_signals.visual_form: Elemental potions scattered throughout levels, combinable for buffs
safety_signals.meaning: Temporary power enhancement; player agency in survivability

faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Traverses all four elemental planes; present across all level types
Appears in plot exposition screens; conceptual antagonist driving narrative

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Bosses and enemies vary by elemental plane affiliation
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Distinct visual theming per plane; environment reflects elemental allegiance

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Four element-based environments (earth, air, fire, water planes) with standard difficulty
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Standard enemy and boss encounters; moderate platforming challenge

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Darker, more intense versions of elemental planes; increased visual complexity
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Considerably harder levels and bosses; heightened threat intensity

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Dark, moody Giger-esque aesthetic throughout; oppressive atmosphere; hourglass motif
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Infinite lives but hub resets on death; repetitive cycle structure mirrors circular curse; final boss cannot grant peace

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Hourglass background without text; minimal visual reward
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: After 15 minutes, single text line appears: 'Not the end'; reinforces Chakan's futility

environmental_storytelling.example: Four distinct environments (earth, air, fire, water) establish world's supernatural structure and cosmic scope
Layered backgrounds using parallax create depth in grotesque, H.R. Giger-influenced scenery; enhances oppressive mood
New weapons appear in first stage of each elemental plane, suggesting environmental adaptation and progression through supernatural realms

environmental_storytelling.technique: Hourglass as narrative and mechanical motif
environmental_storytelling.example: Hourglass appears in UI, plot exposition screens, and final ending background; visually reinforces time pressure and eternal futility
Central hub stage allows player choice of planes, but death returns player to hub; reflects Chakan's lack of linear progress

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Platforming, combat with twin swords, weapon/potion collection
Return to hub stage; no progress loss on individual level but time pressure persists across plane
Patrol and attack patterns vary by elemental affiliation; visual appearance matches plane theme
Distinct attack patterns; defeat clears level and advances plane progression
Appears only in plot exposition; judges Chakan's victory; condemns him to eternal servitude despite winning

notes: Chakan communicates world-state primarily through atmospheric visual design (dark Giger-esque aesthetic) and mechanical structures (hourglass timer, hub respawning, non-linear plane selection). The game's visual language emphasizes futility and circular curse through repetitive challenge structure and the famous 'Not the end' message. Difficulty was intentionally designed to convey 'pain and futility of Chakan's circular existence.' Factions are implicit rather than explicit—elemental evil is spatially divided across planes rather than politically distinct. World-state manifests as a progression from terrestrial to supernatural evil planes, with visual intensity increasing as difficulty escalates.
